title: Use map_size guard for empty map check
prompt: Given: `def empty?(map), do: if map == %{}, do: true, else: false`. The check `map == %{}` is wrong for structs and for non-empty maps with `%{}` patterns. Rewrite as `def empty?(map) when map_size(map) == 0, do: true; def empty?(_), do: false`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.MapCheck do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Needs knowledge of the map_size-in-guard idiom. Sonnet often uses == comparison.
research_citation: georgeguimaraes elixir-thinking: '%{} matches ANY map—use map_size(map) == 0 guard for empty maps'
